title: The One Where Underdog Gets Away

expressions.dialogue: I was wondering, do you think it would be possible if I got a $100 **advance** in my salary?
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 아직 받지 않은 급여나 팁을 미리 받는 것. '선지급'.
expressions.nuance: 직장 초기에 돈이 급할 때 상사에게 조심스럽게 부탁하는 상황에서 쓰인다. 팁에도 동일하게 적용해 'advance on my tips'라고도 한다.
expressions.example: I had an unexpected bill, so I asked my manager for a two-week advance on my salary. → 예상치 못한 청구서가 나와서 매니저에게 2주치 급여를 미리 달라고 부탁했다.

expressions.dialogue: Ok, I, I **hear what you're sayin'**. I'm with you. Um, but I'm trying really hard.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 상대방의 말을 이해하고 인정한다는 뜻. 꼭 동의하는 건 아니지만 '무슨 말인지 알아'라는 뉘앙스.
expressions.nuance: 비판이나 부정적인 피드백을 받았을 때 방어적으로 반응하지 않고 경청하고 있음을 보여주는 표현. 'I hear you'로 줄여서도 자주 쓴다.
expressions.example: I hear what you're saying about the deadline, but we need at least two more days. → 마감 기한에 대해 하는 말은 알겠는데, 최소 이틀은 더 필요해.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Uh-huh. No, I hear what you're saying and-and-and that makes a lot of sense but can I just say one more thing? (Takes out his picture.) Look how pretty!

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: I'm hearing what you're saying, but at our prices everyone needs toner.

expressions.dialogue: Ok, I, I hear what you're sayin'. **I'm with you**. Um, but I'm trying really hard.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 상대방의 말을 따라가고 있다, 동의한다, 이해했다는 표현.
expressions.nuance: 'I understand'보다 더 구어적이고 친근한 표현. 설명을 듣던 중 '응, 그렇구나'라고 맞장구칠 때도 쓴다. 동의와 이해 둘 다 함의한다.
expressions.example: So we skip the first step and go straight to testing. Yeah, I'm with you. → 그러니까 첫 단계를 건너뛰고 바로 테스트로 가자는 거지. 응, 알겠어.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Hey, y'know, I have had it with you guys and your cancer and your emphysema and your heart disease. The bottom line is, smoking is cool, and you know it.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (proud) I know, but, I made a huge mistake. I never should have broken up with her. Will you help me? Please?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: C'mon, you guys, it wasn't that bad. It was better than that thing I did with the trolls, at least you got to see my head.

expressions.dialogue: **We all chipped in**.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 여러 사람이 돈이나 물건을 나눠서 함께 부담하다.
expressions.nuance: 선물 구매나 비용 분담 상황에서 흔히 쓰이는 표현. 'chip in for a gift', 'chip in on the bill' 형태로 자주 등장한다.
expressions.example: Everyone chipped in and got the teacher a really nice gift. → 다들 돈 모아서 선생님께 정말 좋은 선물을 드렸어.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Uh, I will see you... and I'll raise you. (throws chips in pot) What do you say... want to waste another buck?

occ.speaker: Mnca
occ.text: Ok, this is pumpkin pie with mockolate cookie crumb crust. This is mockolate cranberry cake, and these are mockolate chip cookies. Just like the Indians served.

occ.speaker: Eddie
occ.text: Yeah, you know, put chips in it, we'll make like a chip chick.

expressions.dialogue: Well, **I'm off to** Carol's.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 어딘가로 떠난다, 간다는 의미의 구어 표현.
expressions.nuance: 'I'm going to'보다 더 캐주얼하고 자연스러운 작별 인사 표현. 자리를 뜨면서 목적지를 밝힐 때 쓴다.
expressions.example: Alright, I'm off to the gym. See you tonight! → 자, 나 헬스장 다녀올게. 오늘 밤에 봐!

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Are you kidding? I'm trained for nothing! I was laughed out of twelve interviews today.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Oh God, I can't believe I'm even considering this... I'm very very aware of my tongue...

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Uh, I'm sorry, you don't understand, I'm, I'm, I'm a friend of his. We uh, we used to live together.

expressions.dialogue: **As of today**, I am officially Joey Tribbiani, actor slash model.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 오늘부로, 지금 이 순간부터.
expressions.nuance: 어떤 새로운 상태나 역할이 오늘부터 공식적으로 시작됨을 선언할 때 쓴다. 'as of + 날짜/시점' 형태로도 확장 가능하다.
expressions.example: As of today, I'm officially done with social media. → 오늘부로 나 공식적으로 SNS 끊는다.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Oh, no-no, this place is totally healthy! That-this milk is mine. I bought this today, 'cause I was thirsty for milk, y'know. (She takes a swig of it, but has to turn away from him as she makes a face to show that it has gone bad.) Okay, let's go!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: All right, here's a list of things for you to do today. Man, this going to be so great! Thank you so much! All right, I got to go to work I'm delivering twins today, but only one of them is mine! (Exits.)

occ.speaker: Richard
occ.text: (To Joey) Are you a little off today? It's going terribly slowly.

expressions.dialogue: I am officially Joey Tribbiani, **actor slash model**.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 두 가지 역할이나 정체성을 동시에 가진다는 표현. 'A이자 B'.
expressions.nuance: '/'(슬래시) 기호를 말로 읽는 것. 이중 직업이나 정체성을 가볍게 표현할 때 구어에서 자주 쓴다. 자랑스럽거나 약간 우스꽝스러운 어조와 잘 어울린다.
expressions.example: He's a developer slash musician who releases albums on weekends. → 그는 주말에 앨범을 내는 개발자 겸 뮤지션이야.

occ.speaker: Matress King
occ.text: (on TV) 'Despair fills the mattress showroom. My kingdom is suddenly without a queen. I'm so depressed I'm going to slash... my prices!! Check it out! Four ninety-nine for a pillow top queen set! I'm going medieval on prices!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, well look at this kitchen, slash bathroom. Well that's great! Y'know so you can cook while in the tub.

expressions.dialogue: No, I prefer to **keep a safe distance from** all this merriment.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 어떤 것으로부터 거리를 두다, 관여하지 않다.
expressions.nuance: 물리적 거리뿐 아니라 감정적·심리적 거리두기를 표현할 때도 쓴다. Chandler처럼 특정 상황을 피하겠다는 의지를 유머러스하게 표현할 때도 사용된다.
expressions.example: After that drama, I'm keeping a safe distance from anything involving office gossip. → 그 소동 이후로 사무실 뒷담화에는 아예 거리를 두고 있어.

expressions.dialogue: I'm goin' to the roof, **who's with me?**
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 누가 나랑 같이 할래? 누가 동참할래?
expressions.nuance: 무언가를 하려고 할 때 다른 사람의 동참이나 공감을 구하는 표현. 흥분되거나 즉흥적인 제안을 할 때 특히 자연스럽다.
expressions.example: I'm ordering chicken for the whole team. Who's with me? → 팀 전체 치킨 주문하려고. 같이 할 사람?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I just can't believe you're in here. I mean, what am I gonna do without you? Who's gonna crash the embassy parties with me? Who's gonna take me to the Big Apple Circus?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Of course I am. I reject anyone who's crazy enough to actually go out with me, and then I bitch about the fact that there aren't any great women out there.

expressions.dialogue: I gotta tell you. You're the best in the business. — **Get out.**
expressions.speaker: Girl on subway
expressions.meaning: '진짜로? 말도 안 돼!'라는 강한 놀람·불신 표현. 'No way!'와 같은 뜻.
expressions.nuance: 말 그대로 '나가'가 아니라 충격이나 과도한 기쁨을 표현하는 감탄사. 1990년대에 특히 유행했으며 지금도 쓰이지만 약간 레트로한 느낌이 있다.
expressions.example: You got us front-row seats? Get out! When did you manage that? → 앞줄 자리를 구했다고? 말도 안 돼! 언제 그런 거야?

occ.speaker: Marsha
occ.text: He's out banging other women over the head with a club, while she sits at home trying to get the mastodon smell out of the carpet!

occ.speaker: Woman
occ.text: Yeah, well, I had a 24-inch waist. You lose things. Now come on, get outta my way.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Oh.. you don't get it! (Passes out and slumps across her)

expressions.dialogue: **I gotta tell you.** You're the best in the business.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 솔직히 말하면, 진심으로 말하는데 — 강조용 도입 표현.
expressions.nuance: 칭찬이나 솔직한 의견을 전달하기 전에 주의를 끌기 위해 쓰는 구어 표현. 'I have to say'와 비슷하지만 더 캐주얼하다.
expressions.example: I gotta tell you, that interview went better than I expected. → 솔직히 말하면, 그 인터뷰 생각보다 잘 됐어.

occ.speaker: Alan
occ.text: Yeah, I'm sorry too. But, I gotta tell you, I am a little relieved.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I've gotta tell you, Bob is terrific.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Chandler, I gotta tell you, I love your mom's books! I love her books! I cannot get on a plane without one! I mean, this is so cool!

expressions.expression: You don't know what that means to me
expressions.dialogue: Really? **You don't know what that means to me.**
expressions.speaker: Girl on subway
expressions.meaning: 그게 나에게 얼마나 큰 의미인지 당신은 모를 거야. 감사나 감동을 강하게 전달하는 말.
expressions.nuance: 'Thank you so much'보다 훨씬 감정적인 무게감이 있다. 오랫동안 인정받지 못했거나 큰 도움을 받았을 때 쓴다.
expressions.example: You stayed up all night to help me finish this. You don't know what that means to me. → 밤새 이거 마무리하는 걸 도와줬잖아. 정말 얼마나 고마운지 몰라.

expressions.dialogue: So, **on a dare**, I picked paleontology.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 누군가의 도전(dare)을 받아서, 내기로, 충동적으로.
expressions.nuance: 계획이나 진지한 고민 없이 재미나 도전으로 한 행동을 설명할 때 쓴다. 가볍고 유머러스한 이야기 맥락에서 자주 등장한다.
expressions.example: On a dare, she signed up for a stand-up comedy open mic and actually killed it. → 내기로 스탠드업 코미디 오픈 마이크에 신청했는데 진짜 대박이었어.

expressions.dialogue: I got one keyhole and about a zillion keys. **You do the math.**
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 알아서 계산해봐, 결론은 뻔하잖아.
expressions.nuance: 상황을 설명하고 나서 굳이 더 설명하지 않아도 결론이 명확하다는 의미로 쓰는 표현. 때로는 가볍게 비꼬는 어조가 섞이기도 한다.
expressions.example: We have five people and three slices of pizza. You do the math. → 다섯 명인데 피자가 세 조각이야. 알아서 계산해봐.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Well, not so good. Upstate's pretty big, he's pretty small, you do the math.

expressions.dialogue: No, **not even close**. Forget Vail, forget seeing my family.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 전혀 아니야, 한참 멀었어. 목표나 기대에 한참 못 미친다는 강조 표현.
expressions.nuance: 'No'나 'not really'보다 훨씬 강하게 부정할 때 쓴다. 금액, 시간, 능력 등 격차를 강조할 때 모두 사용 가능하다.
expressions.example: Did you finish the assignment? — Not even close. I'm still on the first section. → 과제 다 했어? — 천만에. 아직 첫 번째 항목에 있는걸.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Not that big a deal? It's amazing. Ok, you just reach in there, there's one little maneuver, and bam, a bra right out the sleeve. All right, as far as I'm concerned, there is nothing a guy can do that even comes close. Am I right?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Not even close.

occ.speaker: Benjamin
occ.text: (looks surprised and un-impressed) That's not even kinda close! (Ross looks around confused) Dr. Li, how many graduate students you'd be needing?

expressions.dialogue: **Set another place for** Thanksgiving. My entire family thinks I have VD.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 식탁에 자리(식기)를 하나 더 놓다. 즉, 한 명을 더 식사에 포함시키다.
expressions.nuance: 명절이나 가족 식사 맥락에서 자주 쓰이는 표현. 'set a place at the table'이라고도 한다. 물리적 행동을 넘어 '자리를 내주다'는 상징적 의미도 있다.
expressions.example: My cousin just called — can you set another place at the table? → 사촌이 방금 전화했어. 자리 하나 더 놓아줄 수 있어?

expressions.dialogue: Right before he reached Macy's, **he broke free** and was spotted flying over Washington Square Park.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 속박이나 제약에서 벗어나다, 탈출하다.
expressions.nuance: 물리적 탈출뿐 아니라 관계, 습관, 상황으로부터의 자유를 표현할 때도 광범위하게 쓰인다. 감정적으로 해방감이 강조되는 표현이다.
expressions.example: After ten years at the same company, he finally broke free and launched his own startup. → 10년간 같은 회사에 다니다가 마침내 독립해서 자신의 스타트업을 차렸어.

occ.speaker: Janice
occ.text: I love the way you look every night Chandler! (Monica breaks the kiss and Chandler freezes in terror.) That's why I made you this tape! Happy Birthday! Love Janice!

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Joey, I did not break this! Okay? (He opens the freezer and smells inside and recoils in disgust.) That has been broken for a while.

expressions.dialogue: I have—and **I remember this part vividly**—a mouthful of pumpkin pie, and this is the moment my parents choose to tell me they're getting divorced.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 이 부분을 아직도 생생하게 기억한다.
expressions.nuance: 강렬한 기억이나 감정적으로 각인된 장면을 이야기할 때 쓴다. 'vividly'는 'I vividly remember' 또는 'I remember it vividly'처럼 위치를 바꿔 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: I vividly remember the moment the acceptance letter arrived — my hands were shaking. → 합격 통보가 왔던 순간이 아직도 생생해 — 손이 떨렸거든.

expressions.dialogue: It's very difficult to appreciate a Thanksgiving dinner once you've **seen it in reverse**.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 음식을 토했다는 것을 완곡하게 표현한 유머. '음식이 거꾸로 나오는 것을 보다'.
expressions.nuance: 직접적인 표현을 피하면서 유머러스하게 상황을 묘사하는 Chandler 특유의 블랙코미디 방식. 불쾌한 상황을 간접적으로 표현하는 유사 구조를 만들 때 참고할 수 있다.
expressions.example: After riding that rollercoaster, I nearly saw my lunch in reverse. → 그 롤러코스터 타고 나서 점심을 역순으로 볼 뻔했어.

expressions.dialogue: So why was I busting my ass to make this **delicious** Thanksgiving dinner? — **You call that** delicious?
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 그걸 ~라고 할 수 있어? 그게 ~야? 상대방의 주장이나 묘사에 강하게 반문하는 표현.
expressions.nuance: 상대방의 주장을 비꼬거나 반박할 때 쓴다. 약간 날카롭거나 놀리는 어조가 섞인다. 뒤에 형용사나 명사 모두 올 수 있다.
expressions.example: You call that an apology? You didn't even look me in the eye. → 그게 사과야? 눈도 마주치지 않았잖아.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Chandler, you called Janice! That's how much you wanted to be with someone!

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: If you wanna call that a reason.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Oh. And what do you call that?

expressions.dialogue: So why was I **busting my ass** to make this delicious Thanksgiving dinner?
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 매우 열심히 노력하다, 죽어라 일하다.
expressions.nuance: 비속어이지만 일상 대화에서 꽤 흔히 쓰인다. 특히 노력에 대한 인정을 못 받거나 억울함을 표현할 때 자주 등장한다. 격식 자리에서는 'work incredibly hard'로 대체.
expressions.example: I've been busting my ass on this proposal for a week and the client rejected it in five minutes. → 일주일 동안 이 제안서에 죽어라 매달렸는데 클라이언트가 5분 만에 거절했어.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Thank you. (To Gunther, who's standing there frozen) Okay, go! Go! Go! (He runs off.) (To Larry) Now, if after dinner you still really need to bust someone, I know a hot dog vendor who picks his nose.

expressions.dialogue: I'd like to **propose a toast**. Little toast here, ding ding.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 건배 제안을 하다, 건배사를 하다.
expressions.nuance: 공식적이거나 반공식적인 자리에서 건배를 시작할 때 쓰는 고정 표현. 'raise a toast'도 같은 의미다. 식사 자리에서 주의를 끈 후 쓰인다.
expressions.example: Before we dig in, I'd like to propose a toast to the couple. → 먹기 전에, 두 분을 위해 건배 한마디 하겠습니다.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, I'm putting my foot down. Yeah look, when I proposed I told you that I would do anything to make you happy, and if having the perfect wedding makes you happy then, then that's what we're gonna do.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Look. Look, my mom gave me that ring because she wanted me to propose to Rachel, but all I wanted to do is if she maybe...kinda...wanted ah...start...things up again.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, I'm putting my foot down. Yeah look, when I proposed I told you that I would do anything to make you happy, and if having the perfect wedding makes you happy, then, then that's what we're gonna do.

expressions.dialogue: Shall I carve? — **By all means.**
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 물론이죠, 어서 하세요. 상대방의 제안이나 요청에 흔쾌히 동의하는 표현.
expressions.nuance: 'Sure' 또는 'Go ahead'보다 약간 격식 있고 정중한 느낌. 상대에게 권한이나 허락을 기꺼이 넘겨줄 때 쓴다.
expressions.example: Do you mind if I start without you? — By all means, go ahead. → 먼저 시작해도 될까요? — 물론이죠, 어서 하세요.

expressions.dialogue: No, we were just laughing. You know, how **laughter can be infectious**.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 웃음은 전염된다. 한 사람의 웃음이 주변으로 퍼지는 현상.
expressions.nuance: 'Infectious'는 웃음, 열정, 에너지처럼 긍정적으로 퍼지는 것을 묘사할 때 쓴다. 'infectious energy', 'infectious smile'처럼 폭넓게 확장 가능한 유용한 단어다.
expressions.example: Her infectious laugh had everyone in the room smiling without knowing why. → 그녀의 전염성 강한 웃음에 방 안 사람들이 이유도 모르고 다들 따라 웃었다.

expressions.dialogue: I'm very **thankful that** all of your Thanksgivings sucked.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: ~이어서 감사하다, ~이어서 다행이다.
expressions.nuance: 단순 감사(thankful for + 명사)와 달리 'thankful that + 절'은 구체적인 상황이나 결과에 대한 감사를 표현한다. Chandler의 대사처럼 역설적·유머러스한 맥락에서도 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: I'm so thankful that the rain held off until after the ceremony. → 식이 끝나고 나서야 비가 내려서 정말 다행이었어.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Yeah, you know what we should all do? We should play that game where everyone says one thing that they're thankful for.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Well, at least the perfume is not mine, be thankful for that!
